original_image_tags: Aged, yellowed, torn document page with visible foxing and holes at the top. Text from the reverse side of the page is bled through and visible as mirrored, faint text. A purple stamp is oriented upside down at the bottom center.

The document shows significant bleed-through text from the opposite side of the paper, appearing as mirrored, illegible ghost text across the upper two-thirds of the page. The paper is heavily aged with a yellowish-brown tint, water damage or foxing spots, two punch holes at the top edge, and a torn right edge.

elements.description: Aged, yellowed paper with torn edges and two hole-punches at the top. Contains inverted, faded blue stamped text reading 'MAR 2 1948', 'FEB 21 2 44 PM '48', 'RECEIVED-MAIL ROOM', 'FBI', and 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE'. Faint, illegible text is visible in reverse, suggesting bleed-through from the other side.

judge.reason: Description B quotes specific, structured verbatim text from the stamp ('MAR 2 1948', 'FBI', etc.) which Description A does not mention and instead describes only as a generic 'purple stamp', indicating a direct contradiction on the readability and content of a key visual element.
